input: Create a FamilyDogIllustration showing Maple the golden doodle sitting in a meadow
Add a WinterIllustration for a seasonal home screen variant — snow on the barn roof, bare trees, frozen pond
failures: COMPLETE_SCENE: The output is truncated mid-code — the second bare tree's branches are cut off, the frozen pond rect is missing from the actual SVG (only listed in the budget comment), and the foreground snow rect is also absent. The scene is incomplete as rendered.

input: Add a new MealsIllustration for the Provisions page — a cozy kitchen scene with a cast iron pot, wooden cutting board, and fresh herbs
failures: PAPER_CUTOUT_STYLE: The illustration relies heavily on linearGradients (wallGrad, tableGrad) for the background and table surfaces, which contradicts the flat paper cut-out aesthetic. True paper cut-out style requires solid flat colors stacked at distinct depth planes, not gradient fills.

COMPLETE_SCENE: The SVG code is truncated — the herbs bundle group is incomplete (circle element is cut off mid-tag, JSX is not closed), meaning the component would not compile or render. The scene is unfinished and the canvas is not fully filled with content.

input: Create a SeedlingIllustration for a spring-start widget — small pots in a tray, just sprouting
failures: paper_cutout_style: The illustration uses only simple rectangles and basic paths without the layered paper cut-out aesthetic. There are no distinct depth planes (sky layer, mid-ground, foreground terrain) — just a flat background rect and functional shapes. The tray/pots sit on a plain background with no ground plane, horizon, or layered stacking effect characteristic of paper cut-out style. Fewer than 4 visible color layers are present as depth planes.

color_variety: The palette is limited to warm tans/browns (tray and pots) and greens (stems and leaves) against a cream background. There are no dark palette values (layer1/layer2 deep colors for background depth) and no lighter accent colors (layer5/layer6/layer7 or contrasting warm/cool highlights). The greens are all mid-range with no dark anchoring tones or bright light accents to create contrast range.

input: Create a GreenhouseIllustration — glass panes, seed trays, misting can, tomato plants
